Contenido: okay let begin by saying that i did not have any hype for cyberpunk 2077 when it launched i didnt start hearing about it until all the keanu reeves city to burn memes started popping up and ta make nicheish build and grind for little to time and badabing badaboom done i finished the game at character level 43 and with level 50 street cred i think 50 is max for both but defo is for sc Contenido: if youre looking for more quality reviews like this follow our curator page devils in the detailthe night city of reality the night city of expectationscyberpunk 2077 is game of grandiose ideals that came with grandiose marketing to the extent that it can be chore parsing out the games fluff from its actual content the ad campaign that surrounded the project was so invasive and ubiquitous that even after almost 60 hours of playtime im still not fully equipped to separate what the game is from what it was intended or advertised to be thats not wholly cd project reds fault either i ultimately bear some responsibility for creating something in my mind that developer ever fully realized part of that to do with the high esteem that i hold for their previous projects specifically the witcher 3 so its not completely fair to cd project reds creative and yet it was also their marketing that constructed the hype train i was riding regardless of how we got here the sheer amount of dissonance from what i thought i would play partly my fault from what the game actually became mostly their fault creates tricky ruleset for reviewing aside from the game figuratively crashing headlong into the hurdle of my expectations the game is still sometimes fun its usually charming occasionally dull always buggy relatively beautiful mess of game the exhausting vacillation between positive attributes and negative characteristics makes for rather arduous game of mental tennis few parts of the game come without greatness likewise part comes without lethargy the incongruity between the positive and negative makes reviewer somewhat hesitant to state the game is good or bad or fair but i will with lump in my throat saythe game is ok and when it was over i was ready to move onhttpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2337786881 mistakes were madetheres an rpg problem that exists and its pervasive in two waysfirstly the character you control v is already paintbynumbers character for whom the player will fill in the canvas ive seen this criticized in general sense and im not completely on board thousands of rpgs like this have already existed without complaint geralt from the witcher works in similar fashion you never take the reins of characterization completely this is different aside from book series and three video games worth of establishment from what came before other than it seemingly contrasting from what was advertised but make mistake v is already person and people have problems goals and aspirations you will not decide these for her or in my case him occasionally though there are general inconsistencies with v it seems weird for instance that streetkid would work with police officers and politicians yet does if you allow him to so should i simply not allow it or is it mistake for the developers to have allowed it the onus is ambiguous the answer unfortunately appears to be of consequence v can or cant you decide any streetkid code in your head is just that in your head which makes the roleplaying feel superfluoussecondly the main quest of cyberpunk 2077 is timesensitive not actually but storywise this is an enormous issue it creates story template that conflicts with the players desire to explore if the main quest deadline and that deadline not being met results in something terrible happening why in the world would v if this were real bother doing anything else that cd project red went through the trouble of creating the answer is that v would not because it doesnt make sensehow can cyberpunk 2077 justify that anyone with even slight measure of intelligence particularly its protagonist would go through the trouble of falling in love racing cars beating up nobodys having sex eating drinking or shopping if they had this totally separate issue that renders all of these earthly pleasures utterly meaningless the main story breaks the world httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2337770249 the player simply can not based on the games own story rationalize doing anything other than the main quest and that main quest isnt that long especially in comparison to the amount of side content and because the plot moves so quickly and v so slowly disconnect forms and the plot cant sustain itself you see its possible that v the player will be just too busy messing with hookers or dirt biking in the desert or getting drunk in bar to adhere to the allimportant details of the quest cyberpunk 2077 says is the most important its easy to outroleplay the narrative because any main plot particularly one that is timesensitive simply dissolves in an open world like this basically why give the player tons to do then by the nature of the plot tell them theres time to do any of it because as stated if this were real they surely wouldnt v looks like an idiot for having fun the land of zero gravitydespite the lack of sense it makes for character who should be doing anything but there are lots of fun things to do in night city i was regularly captivated by the vistas the populace the pulsing radio and the night jaunts through the citys underbelly while not being revolutionary i felt this much and more in the fantasy realms within the witcher 3 it still achieved high level of verisimilitude for the city proper theres something to night city even if it was less than advertised many of my favorite moments of the game were the straightaways with my figurative foot on the gas speeding into oblivion it was in these moments when night city felt alivefor few seconds at least until i would suddenly glitch outside my car and be suspended in the air just above it surfing on the hoodbugs and glitches are prevalent i casually surfed on top of my car often you will see anomalies like this everywhere and the real tragedy with these regular irregularities other than their predictability is that they ruin moments that would otherwise work exceptionally well imagine sensitive moment between two lovers or friends or glorious shootout where v against all odds blasts his way through legion of adversaries only to be met with invisible goons random floating objects or decapitated people who are still very much alive despite its potential for danger bugs colored the whole of night city in something goofy and unintimidating its circus clownhouse night city is supposed to be as much of character as v is and either by content or unintentional moments of motosurfing complete with floating inanimate objects or people that goal is never fully realized and that is the biggest shame of cyberpunk 2077httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2337775537 conclusioncyberpunk 2077 does not feel especially innovative or finished which is shocking considering its marketing truthfully it just kind of exists another contemporary neither truly offending nor pleasing owning nothing it really excels at or really even anything its particularly poor at save quality controlits pretty glitchy rpgshooter that exists in an open world it grants the player the freedom of exploration and the freedom to level up via skill tree youll meet some interesting characters do some interesting things and in the end spend 50 hours completing it allsound familiar it should youve been playing it for years610 fair
positive_highlights: ['Night City vistas', 'Interesting characters', 'Interesting things to do']
negative_points: ['Bugs and glitches', 'Main quest conflicts with open world', "Inconsistencies with V's character"]
summary: Cyberpunk 2077 is a glitchy RPG shooter in an open world that ultimately feels uninnovative and unfinished.

Contenido: good railroad story game not so great rpg there will be appropriately tagged spoilers for the ending because for the ending is easily where the game falls apart so i need to address it however dont shy away immediately as some of the ending criticisms are spoiler freefor starters i would personally rate the game as 610 or 710im not joking when i say i would still recommend game that i believe is 610 and this is because overall cyberpunk 2077 is good game my main issue with it is that it is not what i was expecting i wasnt expecting better game but rather different gameanother quick point is that although i have 56 hours in game most of this was leaving my pc on overnight i reached the endgame having done the main quest and side quests with actual story in about 30 hours the pros 1 the world itself for this game is fantastic the dystopian architecture is outstanding i like the clear differences between vibrant night city and the desolate badlands2 adding onto the above is the world building everything that adds life to the city the cars the people the clothing the flashing lights and the advertisements they all fit quite neatly and look great3 the characters in this game are solid too the two that stand out for the most are jackie and judy these two characters in particular are moulded in such way that it genuinely feels like there is relationship between these characters and v even for male v in judys case4 the rpg elements within the game are apparent theyre used frequently to access new dialogue options sometimes ways to finish quest but mostly to open doors but even then it just feels like youve made good build however they arent flawless the cons 1 first up the bugs some of them feel like really simple bugs sometimes theyre game breaking i play bethesda games so im used to bugs but theyre worth pointing out in any review2 the police and crime systems are completely whack when you commit crime you can physically see the police spawn in around you but they dont chase you you can tear off in car and lose 4star rating after driving one or two blocks it just feels like pointless system also you can literally swipe everything not nailed down without consequence3 the driving is something horrific generally the cars feel really floaty and slightly tapping or d will usually send you veering off into poor crowd of civvies bikes handle much better but in comparison to the cars they feel like dead weight not floaty enough plus collisions are nightmare slight tap with some vehicles seems to send you flying all over the place and its just tiring to deal with4 the gunplay feels bit clunky to again ive played the older bethesda fallout games so im used to it but it just simply felt like i couldnt keep up with the ai and it didnt feel like sensitivity issue because my sensitivity felt fine outside of combat add to that guns that just feel really weak in general and youve got system that definitely needs revisiting5 the rpg elements they exist and they allow you to play role yes but its simply astounding just how irrelevant they feel to the actual game there are very few choices i can recall and none of them really bring to mind any particular consequences at any point throughout the game6 the 5050 approach to sandbox cyberpunk is strange in that the world is so beautifully crafted it makes you want to explore however theres nothing to actually do when you do explore there are the gigs but theyre so mindnumbingly dull that they dont feel worth dedicating time to one gig had go into basement and turn on some lights for some eddies7 this sort of ties in with the above but there were very few side quests that were story oriented in my opinion for an openworld rpg i feel like i shouldnt be finishing the game within 30 hours im spoiled by the elder scrolls and fallout even cdprs the witcher games in that i want to have games that i can sink days into at time and still not have finished the few storyoriented side quests contained within are great there just isnt enough of them8 the overall tone of the story i dont feel like im spoiling anything here really so tag the game the player constantly feel like theres sense of rush sense of urgency this leaves the player feeling like theres very little freedom to go and explore this is exacerbated by the fact that even some of the side quests remind you of this overall rushthis shows that the game prioritises its story above all else and this ultimately means is that this isnt cyberpunk the rpg its cyberpunk the railroaded action game its story game with rpg elements just slapped on top the ending more cons for it should go without saying that this will be spoiler territory so be warned ill spoiler tag all of it but im separating my ending review into two sections mechanics and content im torn on leaning towards dislike for the ending and i dislike the way it works separating them into two sections allows those who want to see why i criticise the ending to do so without ruining the actual ending itself for themselves1 the mechanicsa the ending you get at the end of the game is completely determined by single choice you make during the final mission technically two choices but that falls under content so i cant say more here this adds on to the point about the lack of true rpg elements and the lack of consequence b theres also endgame content the free roam simply places you prior to the ending of the final mission and this just leaves feeling robbed of true ending 2 the contenta the actual ending itself just feels terrible you spend the entire game trying to get johnny out of your body only to get told v is going to die in 6 months anyway it just makes feel like ive wasted my time everything just feels robbed from b the endings dont leave any room for dlc expansion post game because most involve v leaving nc or dying dlc wont feel like an extension just more side stuff that tonally doesnt feel like you can fit into vs existing story is cyberpunk 2077 good game yes as long as youre looking for storyled game if youre looking for an rpg with freedom akin to bethesda titles or the witcher 3 change those expectations because otherwise youll just end up being disappointed like i was and it will take you time to truly appreciate this game for what it actually is
negative_points: ['Numerous bugs', 'Poor police and crime system', 'Clunky driving and gunplay', 'Underdeveloped RPG elements', 'Lack of meaningful choices', 'Repetitive side quests']
summary: Cyberpunk 2077 is a good story-driven game but falls short as an RPG, especially regarding player freedom and meaningful choices.

Contenido: heres the straighttothepoint n dirty toptobottom almostentirely spoilerfree version of cyberpunk 2077 at present after 60 hours of playtime ignore the extra 12 paused the game and forgot to shut it down before i went to bed one night is cyberpunk 2077 finished game absolutely not the ammo limit inventory limit and airandomlybreaking bugs are all very small but critical issues that need to be fixed ill expand upon these below is cyberpunk fully playable absolutely is cyberpunk 2077 fun you bet your ass it is is cyberpunk 2077 worth regular price atpresent id say so if youre someone who only plays for the story and doesnt care about doing side missions then youre going to get the absolute minimum out of cyberpunk 2077 as the game been built from the ground up with plethora of extra story content locked behind side mission decisionscompletion and on top of recommending you learn to actually enjoy games properly id also recommend you wait for cyberpunk 2077 to go on sale if thats the case but if you play games properly and enjoy exploring doing side missions and just generally messing about with ooh shiny icons on the map youll absolutely get your moneys worth from cyberpunk 2077 even at its current full price okay so whys everyone complaining about the game so the game some minor issues such as graphical issues during the cutscene sequencesanimations i say cutscene even though the game doesnt have any actual cutscenes to refer to moments where you become lockedinplace and can only look and select dialogue such as when youre with evelyn in judys bd basement theres sequence where evelyn gets out of her chair to walk over to judy only she doesnt actually animate out of the sitting position until shes 75 of the distance through her walk so for the first 34ths of the walk shes doing some harry potter seatedfloating magic or when youre in the taxi with jackie on the way to meet dex after getting shot up during the heist jackie goes to pull the biochip out of his head to give it to you except the animation forgets to remove his pistol so you see literally half of the barrel of his handgun protruding out the opposite side of his head and few more cases like this as the game progresses theres also the aforementioned issues with ammo limitsmanagement you have hardcap of 100 shotgun shells 100 sniper rounds 500 handgun and 700 rifle rounds only the game doesnt actually indicate this anywhere you have to test to figure it out or read other people complaining about it on forums who did their own testing where when you pick up craft or buy ammo above those limits the game will happily take your crafting components or money fill you to the limit and then just vaporize everything above that limit it doesnt drop on the ground it doesnt give you refund it doesnt even give you an error notice just poof already have maximum ammo great youre still able to waste your crafting components money crafting or buying more but its still going to just cease to exist with refund or warningthen the inventory management issue almost identical to ammo management you have maximum carrying capacity based on weight which thankfully can be increased through various means that the game does actually tell you about so whats the problem you can continue to craft pick up equipment above your maximum carrying capacity the game wont give you any notice or error it doesnt just drop excess equipment it just completely erases it found sweet legendary piece of gear in chest and picked it up when your inventory was already full apparently doesnt drop doesnt automatically get decrafted into components doesnt refuse to pick it up or even give you so much as courtesy warning it gets picked up the game realises that it doesnt fit in your remaining inventory space and just completely erases it from the game this is something i wouldnt even expect from firsttime indie developer much less cd projekt redthen theres also the notuncommon issue of enemy ai just completely breaking theyll be fighting running moving doing their thing and then just randomly break they stop moving stop shooting stop responding even if you shoot them or hit them with grenade they basically turn into boxingsparring dollthen lastbutnotleast the broken scrapper perk automatically disassembles all junk into components sounds great right but read on which doesnt have any filter for the valuable junk which is worth 750 and not just 3 and will happily disassemble them as well with way to turn it off besides complete skill reset which costs 100000 seriously guys you couldnt create tagword in the code for jewelry or even just an extra tab called jewelry or valuables to avoid this issue thankfully i had the foresight to avoid this particular issue myself by not getting the perk but ive read legions of lessmindful folks complaining about this on every game forum known to man since the game releasedwhich loops us back to the original why are people complaining long story short for those who dont already know massive expectations were dropped on the actual developers by the suitsupstairs at cd projekt red an unrealistic release date was set by the suits as all the developers flat out said this isnt possible this release date was pushed back several times as the suitsupstairs realized yep theyre right but we can still crunch it to this time period after several delays vocal group of potential consumers complained and whined and cried and pressured cd projekt red into releasing game that they flat out knew wasnt ready only to appease fans who then cried when loandbehold the game wasnt perfect and entirely finished the things they complained about had to be the 3 things i just mentioned because otherwise the game feels complete and most of the negative reviews dont actually cite any specific issue they just cry about how bad the game is its so terrible its not finished okay yes but howanyways the game supposedly based on some online articles cant personally verify if theyre true or not yet but i did see there were multiple possible options to go down different ending paths so theres at least few 6 distinct endings so you get lot of control over how things go down depending on your actions throughout the game mainly choices in certain sidequests as well as having to have completed certain side quests to unlock certain endings the city feels incredibly alive theres people everywhere you see ais regularly talk to eachother and with the infinitelygenerated ncpd combat minimissions and the plethora of sidemissions theres always something to do in almost every corner of the city seriously the only people getting bored in night city are people who either arent into fpss or who dont enjoy games properly and just blatantly ignore side quests and only focus on the main storyanyways thats my bit maybe ill see around at the afterlife kid stay safe out there

Contenido: introductionelden ring is soulslike action rpg by the legendary fromsoftware the developers of the dark souls series where the main goal is to expand the genre by allowing you full access to an open world environment players will have treacherous traps to bypass devious bosses to decimate and epic distances to travel on their path to becoming an elden lord positives negatives classic souls formula where combat prowess determines your progression large selection of weapons and armour the sheer scope of the open world is overwhelming wellwritten and captivating storyline new crafting mechanic is intuitive and easytouse all damage types and builds are viable truly terrible launch where many bugs and issues still exist in the game there is tangible balance between different builds mechanically boring boss encounters an abhorrent amount of recycled content especially in the latter half of the game unannounced patchesfixes completely break builds this forces you to either farm levels to make up the gap or reassign attribute points at your own cost to fix it storyin elden ring you assume the role of warrior derogatorily known as tarnished being whose existence is an affront in this land where gold and power rule all you soon find out that this world been torn apart by countless wars and that very little of its once noble kingdoms remainyou must use sites of grace elden rings bonfire replacement to guide you across these lands and seek out the great rune bearers with the hope that you can eventually restore the elden ring itself and assume the mantle of elden lord but this is simple task as over time these great beings have become lost and confused in powerfuelled madness and they will do anything to ensure the runes remain in their graspelden rings story is mostly masked in fog and intrigue and to truly understand it in its entirety you will likely need several playthroughs and to explore every inch of its world to unravel it what is explained to you however is beautifully written and flows exceptionally well for the entirety of the gamehttpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2790851412gameplay analysisa world where power is taken not giventhe heart of all soulslike games is inevitably the combat where prowess and skill determine whether you are victorious or suffer crushing defeat elden ring is the same for the most part combat is more forgiving than previous fromsoftware entries especially compared to their previous title sekiro where even minor mistakes could cost you dozen hours despite its fairer scenarios you will still require some finesse to progress combat is smooth and for the most part pretty fair although there are some cases where it can feel clunky or just plain brokenyou have far more freedom here to build your character exactly how you want to with limited respecs available to your character as well as plethora of weapons and armour to choose from combine all of this with both the new and improved ashes system where you can enchant nonunique weapon with specific skill and completely rebalanced magic and ranged system you have fairly dynamic gameplay experiencethere is also newly implemented crafting mechanic while i didnt use it to its full potential i can see it being useful to the player who wants an edge for boss fights or difficult encounters most crafting materials are freely available in either the openworld or as drops from specific enemieslike butter spread too thin on toastthis is the best analogy i can give to the games openworld experience in truth the scope feels extraordinary with every visible location in the distance being place worth investigating it saddens that this falls short of expectation as the games format doesnt fit this kind of experience it feels like flattened and rolledout version of the original dark souls formula without updating it all to compensate for the vast distances this is made more evident in the latter portion of the game where its riddled with recycled content bosses and enemies you have already fought will return to the fray with slightly more health or higher damage output which honestly is just lazy game design and it entirely detracts from the atmosphere intendedthere can be victory if the war was lost to begin withveterans of the genre will be glad to see the vast quantity of bosses and enemies this game to offer from monstrous dragons to overwhelming giants this game it all so it disappoints greatly to play through the entire campaign completely unimpressed by the lack of boss mechanics tactics and overall strategy there are obviously exceptions to the rule but the overall experience fighting most of them felt uninteresting and stale the side content isnt much better where you are literally just riding around looking for copypaste dungeons or crafting materials to bolster you against the next boss fightaudio and visualthere can be denying this game is stunning real treat for the eyes in nearly every aspect of its design the world is gorgeous the vistas are cinematic and the bosses are heavily detailed in way telling you story about themselves by just standing before you my only major concern is the sheer amount of golden visuals that encompass your vision matter where you look httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2790850758the audio falls into the same boat with most of it being recycled from previous games it immediately feels little stale there is notion of nostalgia here for sure but i think an entirely fresh experience would have done wonders to contrast this perfectly the soundtrack is just something to behold absolutely one of my favourite soundtracks ever orchestrated with each boss and zone bringing entirely new compositions to the fold and elevating the overall atmosphere pc performance and specificationswith settings set as high as they could go ive had only minor fluctuations in frame rates with my current build below game launch had an astronomical amount of issues some of which were heavily performance based most of these have since been rectified volumes of bugs and crashes experienced in under 100 hours of gameplay some texture tearing and clipping issues were presentram 32gb under load usage 39cpu i9 10900ka 370ghz under load usage 42gpu msi ventus rtx 3080 under load usage 494final thoughts very goodreview chart hereelden ring is wellwritten and gorgeous adventure that also proves without shadow of doubt that you cant simply take an existing linear formula and force it into new openworld shape without it losing some integrity vast array of new and improved mechanics as well as the large catalogue of weapons and armours you can choose from basically ensures that challenge is insurmountable and will keep you build crafting for many hours there were quite lot of bugs and performance issues at launch but these have been rectified for the most part overall decent game and one that definitely caters to larger audience than ever before i can recommend this game in its current state but if you havent been fan of the genre in the past perhaps wait for salefollow our curator page summit reviews to see more highquality reviews regularly
positive_highlights: ['Classic souls formula', 'Large selection of weapons and armour', 'Well-written and captivating storyline']
negative_points: ['Recycled content', 'Mechanically boring boss encounters', 'Unannounced patches/fixes breaking builds']
summary: Elden Ring is a well-written and gorgeous adventure with improved mechanics, but suffers from recycled content and technical issues.

Contenido: good morning night citynight city 2077 you play as v mercenary that wants to be legend in night city and for this to happen youll need to steal the relic biochip invented by arasaka one of the most influential megacorporations in the whole world now that ive just scratched the surface of the plot i can begin with this review city of dreamscyberpunk 2077 the most beautiful city that ive ever seen in video game and at first sight the most vivid city in game history but only at first sight when walking through night city you can see all npcs going on with their lives working shopping or just walking around but of course like in any game it is just an illusion to make the city feel alive however they failed trying to build up this illusion mainly when there is major interaction with the npcs just oneline dialog that repeats over and over again just like in the witcher 3 but here in cyberpunk hits different another thing that killed that illusion for personally was the crowd ai oh boy that ai is terrible when you look back at gta v and older games even and compare its ai with cyberpunk well there is comparison cyberpunk 2077 ai in general are awful and totally immersion breaker and for sure they were big dreamerscontinuing on with the world itself cdpr managed to handcraft beautiful and immersive openworld full of details that you can get lost in its beauty although there isnt lot to do in it in my point of view night city as i said is the most beautiful but at the same time the most empty city that ive seen in the past years because there is absolutely nothing to do in this huge city besides walking and doing some repetitive activities that pop up around the map yet there are random events like weve seen in red dead redemption 2 for example nor extracurricular activities as we see in many other games hell even in gta san andreas 2004 we could go to the gym barbershops casinos even take taxi so on another one of its downsides is the lack of character customization after the character creation for game where style is everything this is huge problem im pretty sure that cdpr planned lot of those activities but to meet the deadlines theyve had to cut it out of the final game now we just need to wait and see if theyre going to implement some of those things in the gameback in 2019 cdpr stated that cyberpunk would have wanted system and corrupt police well at least we got wanted system but not as you expected the police system is just awful literally there is car chase or proper ai for the police they just spawn behind you whatever the place you are at i remember gta 3 from 2001 having better police ai than cyberpunk and thats ridiculousthis is rpg after allat the end of the day cyberpunk 2077 tries to be an rpg but is it really when they first announced the lifepaths i got really excited and even more when i read that every lifepath choice would affect the world around you and that was big fat lie first of all lifepaths just change the game introduction and some minor dialog options and thats it major changes at all about the general choices yes lot of the choices that you made in the main quests or even side quests can have an impact on your ending or at least some important dialogs throughout the story when it comes to rpgs one of the most important things is the skill tree here in cyberpunk we have huge skill tree and it works quite similar to the skyrim skill tree you can upgrade your character to be melee specialist heavy weapon specialist or combine everything in one and be an overpowered one tap machinethe gameplay itself is pretty average nothing spectacular but not bad either we can carry 3 main weapons and toss variety of grenades there are the implants that we can only buy new ones or change the currently equipped on ripper docs the implants that you acquire on ripper docs can play huge role in your gameplay as you can upgrade your max health and carry weight etcthe hidden gem not so hiddenthe best part of the game so far is the main story and the main side quests if i can put it that way cdpr did great job telling the story in way that you dont feel overwhelmed or tired and gets even better when you get to know the characters and their stories you can connect really easily with the characters and thats huge point for in this huge game industry lately theres been lack of good characters that you feel for and cyberpunk 2077 makes you care about the characters even more than you shouldthe soundtrack is impressive even if many of the songs didnt suit my style they did awesome work creating the soundtrack unfortunately the main quests are too short compared to witcher 3 about 20 hours if you rush throughout the game i finished the main plot in 50 hours however half of my playtime was doing side missions and exploring the map ive had so much fun doing side missions and some ofem are i dare say better than some of the main storyrelated quests the not so good the bad and the uglynonetheless the bugs as everyone might know by now cyberpunk 2077 is full of bugs i can make book with five thousand pages about the bugs in my playthrough i didnt encounter any gamebreaking bug thank god but theres lot of glitches for example cars flying around npcs appearing from nowhere and lot of clipping glitches more than usual etc the most annoying bugs are questrelated ones even though i didnt encounter any gamebreaking bugs there are still some glitches that ruined my immersion and i hope that theyll fix those very soonconclusionim really divided about this game in one hand there are the great main storyside quests and the great characters but on the other hand theres lot of bugs glitches and main issues that make this impressive world well less impressive ill recommend cyberpunk 2077 more because of its great story characters and fun gameplay however we should treat this review as mixed one but steam doesnt allow us to do so im looking forward to seeing if cdpr will pull off hello games and make cyberpunk more enjoyable game
technical_issues: ['bugs', 'glitches', 'clipping issues', 'NPCs appearing from nowhere', 'cars flying around', 'quest-related bugs']
negative_points: ['Bugs and glitches', 'NPC AI', 'Police AI']
summary: Cyberpunk 2077 has a great story, characters and gameplay but is heavily let down by bugs, glitches and other issues.

Contenido: spoiler free reviewjust above the averagefor the 95 of my first playthrough if youd ask for point grade of cyberpunk i would give it something around 6510 above average mostly thanks to the story visuals and music that are all part of experience with the game sadly numerous bugs glitches and minor issues sabotaged an otherwise strong 9 or even 95 but then after playing through all possible endings the story part kinda fell apart dropping the six and half even lower just kinda cause three endings were rather decent but then again one strips you off of around 90 minutes of gameplay and then kicks you in the face for choosing it while the others are not really satisfying and there are plenty more which are just bad in my opinion and because of that i felt disconnected from the whole story and even the 6510 i was readying in my head turned into 5and with an exactly average game i thought to myself well do i even recommend cyberpunk 2077 to others after few battles with my thoughts i decided yes yes i wouldcyberpunk 2077 is actually good game shame it is so overshadowed by how many bugs it and how the story let down at the very end what lived up to the hypei am actually surprised how wellcrafted the ingame world is it made immersed from the get go and thats easy task everything from giant skyscrapers to the smallest houses in the badlands is top notch there was lot of care taken in creating night city and its outskirts quite honestly it actually works like real city would i know i know i just got surprised by it just think about how many games have cities that could actually be habited and used not just for show its not created with heavy impact on travelling by car sure the roads are important but if you wanted to stroll through the city on foot youd have proper sidewalks crossings aisles and footbridges for your disposal something eg gta v was lackingthe lighting the sounds everything seems in order for 2077 metropolis and it makes sense too led lights and all that cyberstuff wasnt just put left right and center for the sake of it the cybersolutions have strong reason to be there instead of neondiarrhea led lights are only in places needing an actual highlight not wasting too many resources so worldbuilding is top notchand then music music is fantastic i already fell in love with couple of tracks the selection of genres is wide and well fitting soundtrack payday syndrome its more beneficial to go stealth but music is way better when you go loud energy given to you through the eardrums is just something else something i enjoyed the most the storyquite honest with you i was very surprised by how neatly the story is constructed the prologue itself takes around 6 hours to get through thats insane that time is also used to perfection you get to bond with some characters you find reason to progress the story and most importantly you start finding alliances and rivalries the characters are likable relatable and compelling you decide how you want to progress and whose stories you want to focus on and the main protagonist v genuinely had think of himher as myself which is an enormous amount of fun being able to feel as part of the game so the immersion aspect was unexpectedly well executedwhat did not live its obvious that the hypetrain for cyberpunk 2077 was far beyond anything this game could achieve lets be honest here the hype surpassed even the one for halflife 3 and that was not healthy for the games release which was just full of flops bugs issues glitches not even far into the game it took less than 30 minutes for the first gameplaydisrupting glitch to occur thats basic stuff that just shouldnt pass qa you know what that reminded of the witcher 3 cd projekt red just wasnt that big back then and more importantly they didnt have that impossible to achieve level of hype behind them witcher 3 was just as bugged and it was fixed in weeks time following the release just like cyberpunk was supposed to be i think i have idea what was going on the peoples mind while first setting release date when we got that broken of title on 10th of december by the time of writing this review the game been patched 6 times already and lot of early game issues have been addressed but cyberpunk is still far from perfect it can now be finished though because guess what the first iteration of the game couldnt be completed as the final boss was just impossible to kill fun rightthe amount of things that still need polishing is astounding im not sure how much time will the game need to reach the state at which it can be considered fullystable at least the random crashes dont occur so often anymore the save states dont get corrupted anymore either so heres hoping that cyberpunk 2077 will reach point at which bugs and issues will be thing of the pastdriving in this game is absolutely horrendous its disgusting with all vehicles i know im racing genre veteran but this was just unbearable understeer is all over the place cars and bikes steer like bricks halfsunk in the sand and the only working brake is the handbrake and it doesnt work too well either so the fast travel becomes mustthe rpg aspect of the game had bit wondering multiple endings and choices matter were thrown at way before cyberpunks release and you know what the first one is true second not so much yeah the differences between endings are in most cases like day and night but the way you get there is not as varied as you could hope for your dialogue options especially while talking with johnny silverhand could as well be limited to yes progress it hurst to say this but there are multiple situations which just ask for any other outcome you can be snarky complete jerk very independent person or wholesome guy and still the dialogue goes mostly exactly the same for every approach why make characters love or hate i dont care but let choose the way they will respond next dont let them come back to neutral line after i insulted or complimented them thats not how humans work matter how you look at it its step back from witcher 3what is left to talk about is the endingi want to bash someone for it i am damn glad i took my time before finishing the game cause the endings are just so disappointing almost regardless of which route you choose feels like the writers responsible for how cyberpunk comes to an end were different people than those who designed the rest of the game i have felt so disconnected from everything i have learned about the game and characters portrayed in it that it almost ruined the entire experience for in most endings most because as i said earlier on i can accept three of them accept is not high praisemost of the endings are just illogical weird stupid even and the results of picking those endings just disappointmentin the end i spent lot of time weighing whether or not would i recommend this game to anyone and you know what despite well everything i would i really would with patches coming every other week maybe in some time the game will reach better state but you know what even with those bugs glitches and all the issues you might come across its very lengthy and very fun experience just maybe enjoy your time before reaching the point of return because after that it gets messy
negative_points: ['Numerous bugs and glitches', 'Disappointing endings', 'Horrendous driving']
summary: Cyberpunk 2077 is a good game with great visuals, music, and worldbuilding, but it is overshadowed by bugs, glitches, and a disappointing story ending, although still recommendable.

Contenido: well i did it i finished soulstype game am i glad i did it yes i think so would i go back and do it again if i could probably not not for very very long time anywaysseriously its obviously wellmade high quality game but man does it have some issueswarning this review is more of vent than anything i actually do think its decent game just not worthy of the hype its garnered the combat is based almost entirely around timing and learning enemy attack patterns this isnt problem in and of itself the right kind of player would love learning the precise time to press space against each kind of enemy however i am not that kind of player i like to play basebuilder type games and occasionally mix in some hackandslash rpg and shooter titles when i dont feel like thinking too hard if im going to put in effort to master game i like to do it by planning my moves ahead of time and experimenting with the tools im given not by mastering each inthemoment microtwitch movement if youre like and flipflop between games like team fortress 2 and factorio id recommend staying away from this onehowever i think that the timingbased combat where one of the games first huge problems rears its ugly head for game so heavily based on timing and careful movement it some awfully laggy inputs dodgeroll one of the most important moves in the game delay on it about half second between when you press the button and when your character even starts to move in the middle of this games tightly timed fights thats huge it took hours upon hours of wondering why dodging felt so bad before i realized what was going oneafter looking at some online guides i learned that this delay is apparently part of every souls title and that most players simply consider mastering it to be part of the challenge i suppose theyre right but think about it anyways significant portion of this games challenge finds its source in intentionally poor laggy controlsim also not sure that the developers ever heard the phrase quality of life when designing this game there are all kinds of weird inconvenient things in this game that simply defy explanation why arent the allimportant health and mana potions bound to unique key why can we only use the special effect on our righthand when we can just briefly unequip it to use our lefthand weapon why do equippedbutunused weapons add to your carry weight if youre quick with the menus its perfectly possible to keep your auxiliary weapons hidden in your inventory and then only equip them when needed so why make us deal with the carry weight issue is rapidclicking through menus supposed to be another combat skill we masterand i think i would be amiss if i didnt point out the poor early gameplay progression of sorceryintelligence charactersi picked the astrologer starting class since i felt like playing spellslinger i booted into the game and started looking for better gear but nothing i tried everything i could think of i followed the path the game sent down nothing i headed east to the higher level area new spells new staffs still nothing it was beyond the twenty hour mark that i finally got new staff as random drop from an enemy in the high level area and it was garbage literally worse than my starting weapon unless you happened to be using one or two ultraniche rock breaker spells and i only found them found those hours and hours later stillit was only when friend clued in to their location that i was finally able to get my hands on some better spells although they were actually pretty close to the starting area theres nothing directing you there if you happen to miss the one particular set of ruins where the vendor is hiding youre screwed new spells for ageseven then it was probably past the fifty hour mark that i finally found staff able to replace the starting staff which by then i had upgraded to be better than most nonunique staffs and hours more before i hit the minimum level to use itlet emphasize something my friend told where to get better spells i started this game determined not to look at the wikis i tend to be really bad about that using wikis to look up secrets however this was not the game with which i should have made that resolution because to paraphrase that same friends words this is game meant to be played with the wiki open on second monitornow we reach the crux of the games design flaws this entire game is made of secrets like seriously im not sure that its possible for single player to complete single side quest in this game without looking up instructions on the wiki at least not without spending hours upon hours upon hours scouring every last corner of the map for the next arbitrary npc location and talking with every character second and third times to make sure that some new dialog option hasnt appeared even characters who supposedly actively want your help will barely tell you what they want or even why you should help them eg one character upon first meeting will ask for your help in her war against the erdtree at this point in the game i still barely even knew what the erdtree was other than really tall and glowing there is option to ask her why she wants war against the erdtree or how even her cryptic assassinations quests help to combat the erdtreethat character was nothing but small contributor to what i consider to be the games single biggest problem this game is so cryptic so utterly and incomprehensibly obtuse and obsessed with secrets that i dont think i could have told you what the plot was had i not read it on the wiki everything else i could have forgiven if the story had just yknow made sensewhats your characters motivation when you start the game you are greeted with cutscene instructing you to become elden lord why who is my character why did they just wake up in this temple was the voice in the cutscene something my character experienced as far as i can tell this is the story of some psychopath who wakes up and immediately goes on rampage killing half the remaining lucid people in this undead wasteland and subjugating the other half part of the fun of an openworld game is supposed to be learning about the world meeting its people and seeing how they live in elden ring though the world seems to be nothing more than setpiece pretty backdrop while you ride between dungeon to boss fight to dungeon there are people who like this stuff lore junkies who will spend hours making crazy theory videos on youtube however if after playing through whole game i cant identify any motive other than go kill stuff and get stronger so you can sit on the chair next to the big tree i think there might be problemthe games problems can be summed up in one fight maliketh apparently you fight him to get something called destined death what is that its never explained cryptic lore moves incredibly fast and your attack windows are comically slim janky timings forget malenia this is the real hardest boss in the game however theres secret item that exists specifically to fight him youd be lucky to find the item without going to the wiki and looking it up and even if you did find it youd be hardpressed to figure out how to use it wiki on other screen kills you quick fights tend to last less than minute however the nearest respawn point is thirty second walk away there is reason it couldnt have been right next to the boss door it didnt add any challenge all it did was make the boss take longer quality of lifetldr if you just want to git good at fighting big bosses in pretty landscape this game is for you otherwise maybe dont believe the hype
negative_points: ['cryptic story', 'poor controls', 'poor early gameplay progression of sorcery/intelligence characters']
summary: The game has significant flaws in controls, story and early game progression, making it frustrating despite its pretty landscapes.

Contenido: introductionelden ring is soulslike action rpg by the legendary fromsoftware the developers of the dark souls series where the main goal is to expand the genre by allowing you full access to an open world environment players will have treacherous traps to bypass devious bosses to decimate and epic distances to travel on their path to becoming an elden lord positives negatives classic souls formula where combat prowess determines your progression large selection of weapons and armour the sheer scope of the open world is overwhelming wellwritten and captivating storyline new crafting mechanic is intuitive and easytouse all damage types and builds are viable truly terrible launch where many bugs and issues still exist in the game there is tangible balance between different builds mechanically boring boss encounters an abhorrent amount of recycled content especially in the latter half of the game unannounced patchesfixes completely break builds this forces you to either farm levels to make up the gap or reassign attribute points at your own cost to fix it storyin elden ring you assume the role of warrior derogatorily known as tarnished being whose existence is an affront in this land where gold and power rule all you soon find out that this world been torn apart by countless wars and that very little of its once noble kingdoms remainyou must use sites of grace elden rings bonfire replacement to guide you across these lands and seek out the great rune bearers with the hope that you can eventually restore the elden ring itself and assume the mantle of elden lord but this is simple task as over time these great beings have become lost and confused in powerfuelled madness and they will do anything to ensure the runes remain in their graspelden rings story is mostly masked in fog and intrigue and to truly understand it in its entirety you will likely need several playthroughs and to explore every inch of its world to unravel it what is explained to you however is beautifully written and flows exceptionally well for the entirety of the gamehttpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2790851412gameplay analysisa world where power is taken not giventhe heart of all soulslike games is inevitably the combat where prowess and skill determine whether you are victorious or suffer crushing defeat elden ring is the same for the most part combat is more forgiving than previous fromsoftware entries especially compared to their previous title sekiro where even minor mistakes could cost you dozen hours despite its fairer scenarios you will still require some finesse to progress combat is smooth and for the most part pretty fair although there are some cases where it can feel clunky or just plain brokenyou have far more freedom here to build your character exactly how you want to with limited respecs available to your character as well as plethora of weapons and armour to choose from combine all of this with both the new and improved ashes system where you can enchant nonunique weapon with specific skill and completely rebalanced magic and ranged system you have fairly dynamic gameplay experiencethere is also newly implemented crafting mechanic while i didnt use it to its full potential i can see it being useful to the player who wants an edge for boss fights or difficult encounters most crafting materials are freely available in either the openworld or as drops from specific enemieslike butter spread too thin on toastthis is the best analogy i can give to the games openworld experience in truth the scope feels extraordinary with every visible location in the distance being place worth investigating it saddens that this falls short of expectation as the games format doesnt fit this kind of experience it feels like flattened and rolledout version of the original dark souls formula without updating it all to compensate for the vast distances this is made more evident in the latter portion of the game where its riddled with recycled content bosses and enemies you have already fought will return to the fray with slightly more health or higher damage output which honestly is just lazy game design and it entirely detracts from the atmosphere intendedthere can be victory if the war was lost to begin withveterans of the genre will be glad to see the vast quantity of bosses and enemies this game to offer from monstrous dragons to overwhelming giants this game it all so it disappoints greatly to play through the entire campaign completely unimpressed by the lack of boss mechanics tactics and overall strategy there are obviously exceptions to the rule but the overall experience fighting most of them felt uninteresting and stale the side content isnt much better where you are literally just riding around looking for copypaste dungeons or crafting materials to bolster you against the next boss fightaudio and visualthere can be denying this game is stunning real treat for the eyes in nearly every aspect of its design the world is gorgeous the vistas are cinematic and the bosses are heavily detailed in way telling you story about themselves by just standing before you my only major concern is the sheer amount of golden visuals that encompass your vision matter where you look httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2790850758the audio falls into the same boat with most of it being recycled from previous games it immediately feels little stale there is notion of nostalgia here for sure but i think an entirely fresh experience would have done wonders to contrast this perfectly the soundtrack is just something to behold absolutely one of my favourite soundtracks ever orchestrated with each boss and zone bringing entirely new compositions to the fold and elevating the overall atmosphere pc performance and specificationswith settings set as high as they could go ive had only minor fluctuations in frame rates with my current build below game launch had an astronomical amount of issues some of which were heavily performance based most of these have since been rectified volumes of bugs and crashes experienced in under 100 hours of gameplay some texture tearing and clipping issues were presentram 32gb under load usage 39cpu i9 10900ka 370ghz under load usage 42gpu msi ventus rtx 3080 under load usage 494final thoughts very goodreview chart hereelden ring is wellwritten and gorgeous adventure that also proves without shadow of doubt that you cant simply take an existing linear formula and force it into new openworld shape without it losing some integrity vast array of new and improved mechanics as well as the large catalogue of weapons and armours you can choose from basically ensures that challenge is insurmountable and will keep you build crafting for many hours there were quite lot of bugs and performance issues at launch but these have been rectified for the most part overall decent game and one that definitely caters to larger audience than ever before i can recommend this game in its current state but if you havent been fan of the genre in the past perhaps wait for salefollow our curator page summit reviews to see more highquality reviews regularly
positive_highlights: ['Classic Souls formula', 'Large selection of weapons and armour', 'Well-written and captivating storyline']
negative_points: ['Recycled content', 'Mechanically boring boss encounters', 'Unannounced patches/fixes break builds']
summary: Elden Ring is a well-written and gorgeous adventure with new and improved mechanics, but suffers from recycled content and some technical issues.

Contenido: upon booting up elden ring youre immediately given glimpse of what is to come through the brilliant soundtrack starting off with few disparate calm strings the main theme slowly morphs into an epic melody of grandeur valor and unrelenting ambition despite having already heard this track in trailers i was blown away shattered even i knew i was in for something special blissful explorationwhereas previous soulsborne titles had you trudging through claustrophobic sewers and maddening towns elden ring is far bigger more ambitious adventure reaching across numerous regions the lands between are massive and filled with things to discover even after 20 hours i was still finding new things in the first area perhaps i had missed cave tucked away in canyon or failed to peruse nearby forest once i even stumbled upon an expansive underground area that you could easily miss its magical really much of the wonder comes from the design ethos gone are the myriad of question markers and intrusive quest logs you would find in other titles instead replaced by something as simple as your own curiosity fromsoftware uses this as tool to compel you forward when you see fort in the distance you will feel urged to go there and the best thing is that it was you and not the game that decided so elden ring relies on your participation and i love that httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2790286880as you traverse these lands its hard not to be impressed by the topography uneven hills and deep gorges combine to create world that can not simply be navigated by holding down your left joystick its gargantuan structures serving not just to undermine you but also as waypoints on your journey it made realize how flat and easygoing the landscapes in open worlds often are in the lands between even the geography is your adversary constantly bending and defying rules my first 30 hours with elden ring were some of the best ive had in gaming and i was ready to name it fromsoftwares magnum opus but then realization dawned wait havent i seen this church beforethere are many reasons to love the soulsborne titles but one of them is the handcrafted quality that permeates throughout every corner of the world feels like it was designed with specific purpose in mind one that you would probably understand through your ponderings and each encounter offered new challenge these were games that not only encourage you to explore but also feature enough secrets to warrant doing so this still rings true here but for every two surprises there was moment of utter disappointment reused structures and bosses are frequent to the point where every foe not overtly important to the narrative will be fought multiple times it also undermines the sense of discovery since you can only happen upon so many identical dilapidated churches before you wonder if its worth it this is truest for the many dungeons and caves they all have the same visual identity and rarely differ in terms of enemies and i have hard time naming just one of them since they all blend into one large portion of copypaste tedium eventually i began looking at these as optional bonus levels rather than integral parts of the world and honestly i think that is the best approach it is also in this repetition that the developers misunderstand what made their bosses so special in the first place like telling joke these encounters require careful buildup harrowing walk through town gone mad or slowly making your way up castle the bosses were never the main attraction they served as final sendoffs stages of thrilling and heartpounding action to juxtapose the relatively quiet exploration this still applies to the linear locations but once you step into the open world miyazaki starts hurling bosses at you like hes kid training for sports match thats not to say the boss fights are bad when you come across one of the more sophisticated fights and composer yuka kitamura starts sending symphonic shockwaves through your ears this game is delightful with few exceptions the mandatory encounters are consistently astounding and satisfyingly grueling rounded off by exquisitely rich and repulsive visual design these moments are truly special httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2790285862elden ring keeps on going just when you think youve seen it all it turns out theres still another half of the story left had it featured enough content to sustain this length it couldve been monumental achievement unfortunately that is not the case theres an entire region where the only unique foe is the boss at the end the rest being awkwardly recycled enemies and one of the best places in the game suffers from similar issueyet i cant help but love elden ring for weeks i was enthralled by its world and when i wasnt playing it i was thinking about where to explore next or how to approach the boss i was stuck on when i present these criticisms of the game its not because i despise it its because i care had this been disappointment i wouldve just shrugged and thought little more of it beauty and hostilitythrough the lands between fromsoftware toys with an interesting dichotomy while the landscapes are gorgeous and downright breathtaking they are far from safe mercenaries in tattered armor oversized crustaceans draconian soldiers the size of giants and even living jars everyone and everything is out to kill you even if there arent many enemies specific to one region there are still plenty of creative enemy designs the open world also gives you lot of freedom in how you approach these you could sneak your way through an enemy camp get up close and personal with comically large greatsword or ride in on your horse slashing wildly at anyone in your way this makes the notorious difficulty far more approachable and id imagine that more people will finish the game because of it the combat system itself is akin to dark souls 3 but there are enough additions to make it not feel worn out and dreary sekiros posture system where an enemys stance can be broken to leave them vulnerable been subtly integrated the enemies dont have wavering stamina bars over their heads but this means you arent pressured into style of play the stance mechanic is there you can use it if you want but it can also be ignored entirely httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid2790286283when combined the stance system and guard counter result in battles coming across more as duels than the old hitandrun affair there were times where i had to run away either to heal or get break from the heat of battle but i spent considerably more time facing my enemy headon you could argue that not enforcing these improvements leads to some players resorting to the triedandtested tactics but i like the freedom it brings another enemy you will face is the performance while the game runs at 60fps most of the time there are some drastic problems with frame pacing and stuttering i didnt find it too distracting but those on lowerend pcs might be more affected its hard not to compare elden rings selection of enemies and locations to previous fromsoftware offerings but part of believes we should look at it through different lens for while it is like these in certain departments its also title that seeks to provide different experience one much more flexible in its design contours and of much grander scale rather than scold it for the ways it compares we should appreciate it for the ways it differs visit cup of thoughts for subjective reviews with touch of personality
negative_points: ['Reused structures and bosses', 'Repetitive dungeons and caves', 'Inconsistent boss encounters in open world']
summary: Elden Ring offers a grand and enthralling open-world experience with challenging combat and exploration, but suffers from repetitive elements and performance issues.

Contenido: cyberpunk 2077cybermeme 2077what can there be said about the disaster of release that was cdprs hotly anticipated follow up to one of the best games of the last generation the witcher 3 wild hunt that hasnt already been said and then regurgitated ad infinium on various sites and message boards the internet over i initially had joke review in the review section for this game which i am replacing with this one because at the time of posting that review cyberpunk 2077 had become one giant meme not place i would have expected one of the most hotly anticipated and most talked about games in generations to land i know cdpr been tolling away hard at work trying to deliver on the game they promised but the reality is that cyberpunk 2077 destroyed lot of trust and goodwill the company had built up over the years with its followers and fellow gamers and i for one will forever look at them with the eyes of sceptic lot of cyberpunk 2077s problems stem from its core cyberpunk 2077 is technically not much of an rpg at all which is disappointing given the table top source material but more of an action fps with light rpg elements many of its interconnected systems either fail to make an impression or simply dont work point in case i couldnt use single replenishable item in my inventory for my entire playthrough which begged the question why was i even bothering collecting all that shtat least on the pc it ran pretty well and while not quite as cutting edge as many believed it would look in the graphics department if taken as whole it could look impressive at times i did find the main story line gripping and most of the side quests were pretty enjoyable to complete when i had convinced myself i was playing an action fps and not an rpg at all i did feel like the end of the game was incredibly rushed as this was where i acutely felt that the team had simply run out of time and couldnt deliver on the ending they had originally planned especially after the boilerplate missions that ratchet up the tension before entering into the final mission the end felt almost anticlimatic because it was over in such jiffy the final boss felt like joke given that we had been built up to fear him and ended up being complete pushoverat the time of play i did initially find all the various graphical glitches quite hilarious but in the end it did start to subtract from my experience i cant speak for the game now october 2021 as it would require replay but hopefully most of those have been sorted out by now whoever decided that crouch and skip scene should be mapped to the same button really didnt play test the game properly because they would have immediately seen how annoying this is when you accidentally press the b button on the controller thinking you are gon na stand up and then end up skipping cut scene the traffic system and ai police npc enemy needed some serious attention hopefully that also been addressed now also cyberpunk 2077 open world didnt quite revolutionise the genre in the same way the witcher 3 wild hunt had done prior that was disappointingif you temper your expectations cyberpunk 2077 can be an awesome thrill and there are genuine moments where you can see the brilliant game waiting to be unearthed lying underneath its mess of systems and visual bugs and glitches the sad thing is with probably another two years development cdpr would have had the classic game everyone and his dog thought they were delivering because the puzzle pieces are all there they just needed to be joined correctly i do believe it could have been that genre defining game that all other games aspire to be like had it been given more time in development so it was sad to see how it got kicked out the door in such poor state most people would have been fine with further delays well at least the adults among us who i like to think outweigh the emotionally stunted death threat script kiddies living in moms basement because we have jobs families other hobbies that also keep us busy we dont have time to tweet sht i get that there are investors and that they want results and they want to see their investment materialise but the 8 million preorders should have been enough of sign that demand was incredibly high for this title sure some might have cancelled their preorders off another possible longer delay but excellent reviews across all platforms would have driven them all back one to ask was this all really worth it as miyamoto put it rushed game is forever bad delayed one is eventually good those should be words you live by in this industry in the end while i thoroughly enjoyed my time with cyberpunk 2077 the fact of the matter is given the hype and the marketing campaign i fully well expected cyberpunk 2077 to come in as my favourite game of 2021 but sadly that not been the case in fact come year end it might not feature in my top 10 games played for 2021 at all i cant help feeling pang of disappointment about this especially given that the witcher 3 wild hunt ended at 1 back in 2015one word or phrase to sum up the experiencefrustratingly disappointing because there is great game in there waiting to emergeperformancebug related incidentsi am sure most of the visual glitches have been sorted now but at one point they were so bad they were subtracting from the experience i never had any quest bug out but lot of the rpg systems put into the game simply didnt work or were half bakedsimilar to gamesthe witcher 3 wild hunt deus ex human revolution watch_dogs legionrating httpssteamcommunitycomsharedfilesfiledetailsid869453510
positive_highlights: ['Gripping main storyline', 'Enjoyable side quests', 'Potential for a great game']
negative_points: ['Rushed ending', 'Disappointing RPG elements', 'Numerous bugs and glitches']
summary: Cyberpunk 2077 is a frustratingly disappointing experience due to its rushed development, numerous bugs, and unfulfilled potential, despite having a gripping storyline and enjoyable side quests.
